verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family tyrosine kinase that is dually acylated (myristoylated at Gly2 and palmitoylated at Cys3), anchoring it to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane — making it inner-leaflet-anchored by default. However, LYN is also well-documented to associate with lipid raft microdomains and is co-immunoprecipitated with surface receptors (BCR, FcεRI, CD19), and importantly, palmitoylation-dependent membrane association places it at the cytoplasmic face, not the extracellular face. No extracellular domain exists. The protein body is cytoplasmic-face-anchored; it is not accessible from the extracellular side. The dominant biology is inner-leaflet anchoring — surface-directed therapeutics target associated receptors, not LYN itself. Verdict: no / inner_leaflet_anchored.
